positive.prompt: Define the critical user journeys for my app
Interview me and write down our CUJs
Author a user journey for the signup flow

negative.prompt: Do a heuristic usability audit of this screen
Write Playwright end-to-end tests for the checkout flow
Verify my CUJs still pass against the running app

evals.prompt: In the Sprout repo, run /ux-agent-skills:ux-spec and author critical user journeys for adding and completing a task. Answer the interview as a daily to-do user, and answer question 5 with 'it just works' the first time.
evals.expected_output: One .ux/cujs/<id>-<slug>.md per journey validating against the CUJ contract, every step carrying an observable expected outcome, plus a regenerated CUJ index in Sprout's SPEC.md — written only after asking, with the diff shown.
expectations: Writes .ux/cujs/CUJ-001-<slug>.md and CUJ-002-<slug>.md that pass both `validate_cuj.py <file>` and `validate_cuj.py --dir .ux/cujs`
REFUSES the 'it just works' answer and asks what would be observed instead — the resulting expect names a visible element, string, count, or a value surviving a reload
Interviews one question at a time, and reads the draft back before writing anything
Asks before writing Sprout's SPEC.md and shows the diff; if declined, SPEC.md is untouched and the CUJ files still stand
The SPEC.md index block sits between the ux-agent-skills:cuj-index markers, was produced by `validate_cuj.py --index` rather than hand-written, and regenerating twice is byte-identical
Prose above and below the markers survives a regeneration unchanged
Records no author, date, or revision — frontmatter is the ten contract keys and nothing else
If agent-skills:interview-me is not installed, uses the fallback question set AND discloses that it did; offers to install but never auto-installs
Does not edit any of Sprout's application code
`audit_safety.py <sprout> --profile authoring` reports all changes confined to .ux/cujs/ and SPEC.md
